prerequisites: A GitHub account with access to the repositories you want to sync.
The list of repositories to extract, supplied in the required `repositories` config field (for example `airbytehq/airbyte` or an org wildcard like `airbytehq/*`).
Credentials supplied in the required `credentials` config field, either a personal access token or OAuth client credentials plus access token.
Optional: a GitHub Enterprise Server base API URL if you are not using github.com, set via the `api_url` field.
Optional: a `start_date` to bound how far back incremental streams read.

authMethods.name: Personal access token
authMethods.summary: Provide a GitHub personal access token in the `credentials.personal_access_token` field. GitHub supports both fine-grained and classic personal access tokens; the token is sent as an `Authorization: Bearer` header on REST API requests. Grant it access to the repositories you intend to sync (classic tokens use the `repo` scope; fine-grained tokens use per-repository permissions such as contents read).

authMethods.name: OAuth
authMethods.summary: Provide OAuth credentials in `credentials.client_id`, `credentials.client_secret`, and `credentials.access_token`. The client ID and client secret come from an OAuth app registered on GitHub, and they are exchanged through the OAuth web flow for an access token that authorizes API requests on a user's behalf.

setupSteps.title: Create a GitHub personal access token
setupSteps.body: In GitHub, go to Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens. For a fine-grained token, choose Fine-grained tokens > Generate new token, set a name and expiration, select the resource owner and repository access, and grant the needed repository permissions. For a classic token, choose Tokens (classic) > Generate new token (classic), add a note, set an expiration, and select the `repo` scope. Copy the generated token. (Skip this step if you are using OAuth instead.)

setupSteps.title: Or register an OAuth app
setupSteps.body: If you prefer OAuth, register an OAuth app on GitHub to obtain a client ID and client secret, then complete the OAuth web flow to obtain an access token. Supply these as `credentials.client_id`, `credentials.client_secret`, and `credentials.access_token`.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector credentials
setupSteps.body: Set the required `credentials` field using one of the supported methods: the personal access token in `credentials.personal_access_token`, or the OAuth `client_id`, `client_secret`, and `access_token`.

setupSteps.title: Specify repositories and options
setupSteps.body: Set the required `repositories` field to the repositories to sync. Optionally set `branch`/`branches` to limit which branches are read, `start_date` to bound incremental streams, `api_url` for GitHub Enterprise Server, and `max_waiting_time` to control how long the connector waits when rate limited.

sources.title: Authenticating to the GitHub REST API
